summary: A recorded local Fox 5 Las Vegas news package, posted by DHS, reporting on the effect of the government shutdown on TSA officers working without pay. The footage includes a close-up of a Transportation Security Administration shoulder patch on a blue uniform with a high-visibility vest, and an on-camera interview with a man identified by a chyron as Don Forman, owner of United Nissan dealership, standing in front of 'TSA SUPPORT REGISTRATION' banners. Lower-third chyrons read 'UNITED NISSAN PROVIDES TSA WORKERS WITH GIFT CARDS' under an 'ONLY ON FOX5 / CONTINUING COVERAGE' label with a Fox 5 bug. The narration describes TSA workers relying on donations during the shutdown. The audio is broadcast reporting speech.

script.scene: Close-up of a Transportation Security Administration (U.S. Department of Homeland Security) shoulder patch on a blue uniform sleeve beside a yellow-green high-visibility vest. Chyron: 'UNITED NISSAN PROVIDES TSA WORKERS WITH GIFT CARDS'; ONLY ON FOX5 label.
A man in a blazer over a white shirt speaks on camera in front of 'TSA SUPPORT REGISTRATION' banners; chyron identifies him as 'DON FORMAN / OWNER, UNITED NISSAN DEALERSHIP'.

notable_text: TRANSPORTATION SECURITY ADMINISTRATION; UNITED NISSAN PROVIDES TSA WORKERS WITH GIFT CARDS; ONLY ON FOX5; DON FORMAN OWNER UNITED NISSAN DEALERSHIP
